brand.title: ESource AI Group (Production)
brand.subtitle: Knowledge Check — CTFS for Account Executives: From Discovery to Deal

questions.question: What are the five financial functions that CTFS unifies into a single platform?
choices: Budgeting, contracting, forecasting, site payments, and participant payments
Budgeting, invoicing, forecasting, vendor payments, and payroll
Contracting, compliance, forecasting, auditing, and reporting
Budgeting, compliance, payments, analytics, and forecasting
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—these five functions running on shared data across one platform is the core CTFS differentiator.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The five functions are budgeting, contracting, forecasting, site payments, and participant payments. CTFS unifies these specific areas because they were previously fragmented across separate tools with no shared data.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes Gilbert's primary pain point?
choices: His AI pilots are stalling before reaching enterprise scale
He lacks unified financial visibility across trials and his teams spend too much time reconciling systems
His tech stack is complex and he fears disruptive implementations
He does not know when payments are coming and invoicing is manual and error-prone
questions.feedback_correct: Gilbert is the Clinical Operations Director, and fragmentation—the absence of a unified view—is his core frustration.
questions.feedback_incorrect: That pain point belongs to a different persona. Gilbert's challenge is fragmentation and lack of visibility; he needs an end-to-end platform with global and multicurrency readiness to reduce manual reconciliation.

questions.question: According to the course, what is the delay cost statistic for a late-stage trial that you should use to open discovery?
choices: Up to $25,000 per day
Up to $55,000 per day
Up to $75,000 per day
Up to $100,000 per day
questions.feedback_correct: The $55,000 per day figure is one of your three key urgency anchors and lands hardest because it is immediate and quantifiable.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The specific statistic from the course materials is $55,000 per day for late-stage trial delays. This number is designed to be memorable and actionable in discovery conversations because of its concrete, high-impact nature.

questions.question: What is the largest benchmark dataset that gives GrantPlan its competitive advantage in budget accuracy?
choices: 12 million negotiated data points
25 million negotiated data points
38 million negotiated data points
50 million negotiated data points
questions.feedback_correct: The 38 million negotiated data points benchmark dataset is GrantPlan's key differentiator and no competitor comes close to it.
questions.feedback_incorrect: GrantPlan's competitive advantage comes from 38 million negotiated data points—the largest benchmark dataset in the industry for clinical trial budgeting. This precision is what enables superior budget accuracy.

questions.question: What does Reena, the VP of IT Clinical, need to hear about CTFS to be convinced?
choices: That it will reduce her team's manual work and provide faster payment visibility
Architectural specifics including API-first, cloud-native design, GxP compliance, and standards-based integration with EDC, CTMS, and ERP
That it is a scalable AI infrastructure layer that connects multiple agents through APIs
That it will accelerate trial timelines and recover revenue from delays
questions.feedback_correct: Reena manages a complex, regulated tech stack and needs technical assurance—API-first architecture, compliance certifications, and integration standards—not feature benefits.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Reena's pain is high maintenance cost and integration risk, not timeline acceleration or payment visibility. She needs to understand the technical architecture, compliance certifications, and integration standards so she can trust CTFS in her environment.

questions.question: According to the course, what market shift should you reference when closing a conversation to establish social proof?
choices: Forty-nine percent growth in trial sites since 2019
Sixty percent increase in protocol amendments
Fifty-eight percent of sponsor executives already use IQVIA Technologies for clinical trial financial management
Ninety percent of the top 20 pharma companies use IQVIA for clinical trials
questions.feedback_correct: The 58% statistic from Frost & Sullivan (May 2025) is the market direction proof point that shows peers have already moved, making it the ideal close-moment stat.
questions.feedback_incorrect: While all these statistics are true, the one to use specifically when closing is the Frost & Sullivan stat about 58% of sponsor executives already using IQVIA Technologies. It demonstrates that the market has made a directional choice, which is powerful social proof at the close.

questions.question: How should you counter Andres's objection that 'we already tried an AI pilot and it did not scale'?
choices: Explain that CTFS is built on more advanced AI than competing solutions
Ask if the pilot had an enterprise orchestration layer, then position CTFS as the infrastructure that makes pilots scalable
Offer to run a smaller pilot with less scope to minimize risk
Promise that this time the implementation will be faster and cheaper
questions.feedback_correct: You counter with a diagnostic question first—did they have orchestration?—then reframe CTFS not as another pilot but as the infrastructure fix for why the last one failed.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The effective counter to a stalled POC objection is not to promise a better pilot, but to diagnose why the last one failed. CTFS addresses the root cause by providing enterprise orchestration—APIs, webhooks, cross-agent coordination—that standalone pilots lack.

questions.question: What are the three inputs to the Total Net Value formula presented in the course?
choices: Platform cost, implementation time, and ongoing maintenance fees
Revenue uplift, cost savings, and solution cost
Trial delay risk, staff efficiency gains, and technology investment
Delay cost per day, number of trials, and team size
questions.feedback_correct: Revenue Uplift plus Cost Savings minus Solution Cost—this formula is the framework for every quantified value proposition in a CTFS pitch.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The Total Net Value formula has three specific pillars: Revenue Uplift (from accelerated timelines), Cost Savings (from reduced manual labor), and Solution Cost (what you are asking them to pay). These three inputs produce the defensible number that moves decisions.

questions.question: If you are entering a sales conversation with both Gilbert and Reena present, what should be your opening strategy?
choices: Lead with the end-to-end platform story since Gilbert is the higher-ranking decision maker
Start with the shared pain (fragmentation and cost of delay) first, then branch to individual value drivers for each buyer
Focus entirely on API architecture and IT compliance to satisfy Reena
Ask Reena to leave the call so you can have a cleaner conversation with Gilbert
questions.feedback_correct: With a mixed buying committee, you anchor in the shared pain every buyer feels, then differentiate by persona as the conversation develops.
questions.feedback_incorrect: A mixed buying committee requires a different approach than a single persona. Start with the universal pain—fragmentation and delay costs—that both Gilbert and Reena recognize, then tailor your proof points and solutions to their individual concerns as the conversation branches.

questions.question: Which persona is looking for CTFS to provide predictability and automated invoice creation tied to CTA terms, and what case study best illustrates her value?
choices: Andres seeking orchestration infrastructure; no case study is mentioned for him
Reena seeking integration validation; the Medidata comparison is most relevant
Rachel seeking cash flow predictability and automated invoicing; the University of Chile Phase III case study managing 8,000 visits
Gilbert seeking unified visibility; the 50% reduction in manual reconciliation case study
questions.feedback_correct: Rachel is the Site Operations Director running lean, and the University of Chile case study—8,000 visits managed with dramatic improvements in speed and accuracy—directly proves her value.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Rachel's pain is cash flow and payment predictability, not platform architecture or orchestration. The University of Chile Phase III case study is specifically called out as the proof point for her value because it demonstrates exactly what she needs: visibility, speed, and accuracy in payment management at scale.
